insights.insight: The compensation distribution is left-skewed (mean $110,575 is below median $111,252), indicating a tail of lower earners pulling the mean down.
The Chicago Police Department employs 38.5% of the city workforce (12,315 employees), making it by far the largest single employer — larger than the next 3 departments combined.
The top 5 departments account for 75.9% of total payroll expenditure, representing a high concentration risk. Budget changes in CPD or CFD alone can materially shift citywide payroll figures.
2,563 employees (8.0% of workforce) earn over $150,000 annually, collectively representing $428.7M in annual payroll.
Part-time employees (n=887) have a median annualized compensation of $19,344 — 83% lower than full-time workers ($112,104). This gap is largely driven by reduced hours rather than lower hourly rates.
DEPARTMENT OF BUILDINGS has the highest median compensation at $133,432, 19.9% above the citywide median — suggesting specialized technical skills command a significant premium.

IQR-based outlier detection flags 729 high-compensation outliers (above $171,633) and 1,321 low-compensation outliers (below $53,001). High outliers are predominantly senior executives; low outliers appear to be part-time/stipend roles.

Consistency.detail: 0 records where pay type doesn't match compensation columns

Uniqueness.detail: 361 duplicate employee names (1.1%) — no unique ID in source

Timeliness.detail: Source data updated annually; no record-level timestamp available
